title: Hedyotis tetrangularis
intro: Hedyotis tetrangularis, also known as Ru Bao or Four Angle Plant, is a perennial herb native to China, traditionally used in medicine for its therapeutic properties. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory infections, as well as promoting wound healing and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Hedyotis tetrangularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to make a decoction, or as a dried powder to be taken as a capsule or tablet, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Hedyotis tetrangularis is classified within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family consists of approximately 600 genera and 13,000 species of flowering plants. The genus Hedyotis is native to Asia and Australia, with Hedyotis tetrangularis specifically found in tropical regions. It is a small shrub with yellow or white flowers and has been used in traditional medicine. The classification of Hedyotis tetrangularis within the Rubiaceae family highlights its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants in this family.
uses: Hedyotis tetrangularis is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, to treat various health conditions. Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In some regions, it is used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and constipation, due to its purported ability to soothe the stomach and intestines. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Hedyotis tetrangularis for medicinal purposes.

Preparations of Hedyotis tetrangularis are often made into teas or infusions, allowing the active compounds to be easily absorbed by the body.
